zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Stewart於1378年出生在蘇格蘭培爾圖利巴迪時，她的父親John Stewart Laird of Lorn28歲，母親Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne17歲。她於1394年與Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine在蘇格蘭培爾培斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1460年10月26日在她的家鄉享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Stewart于1378年出生在苏格兰培爾圖利巴迪时，她的父亲John Stewart Laird of Lorn28岁，母亲Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne17岁。她于1394年与Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine在苏格兰培爾培斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1460年10月26日在她的家乡享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Stewart was born in 1378, in Tullibardine, Perthshire, Scotland, her father, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, was 28 and her mother, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, was 17. She married Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine in 1394, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 26 October 1460, in her hometown, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Stewart est née en 1378, à Tullibardine, Perthshire, Écosse, son père, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine en 1394, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 26 octobre 1460, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Stewart 1378 in Tullibardine, Perthshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Stewart Laird of Lorn 28 und ihre Mutter Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne 17. Sie hat 1394 in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 26. Oktober 1460 im Alter von 82 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Stewart è nata il nel 1378 nel Tullibardine, Perthshire, Scotland, sua padre, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine nel 1394, nel Perth, Perthshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 26 ottobre 1460, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 82 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Stewartが1378年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアトゥリバーディンで生まれた時，父John Stewart Laird of Lornは28歳，母Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorneは17歳であった。1394年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスでSir David Murray VI of Tullibardineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1460年10月26日に故郷で82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Stewart(이)가 1378년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 툴리바딘에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Stewart Laird of Lorn(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1394년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1460년 10월 26일 그녀의 고향에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Stewart nasceu em 1378, em Tullibardine, Perthshire, Scotland, seu pai, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine em 1394, em Perth, Perthshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 26 de outubro de 1460, em sua cidade natal, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Stewart родилась в 1378 г. в местности туллибардаин, пиртшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени John Stewart Laird of Lorn было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine в 1394 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 26 октября 1460 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 82 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Stewart nació en 1378, en Tullibardine, Perthshire, Scotland, su papá, John Stewart Laird of Lorn, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Isabel Ergadia Macdougall - Countess of Lorne, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Sir David Murray VI of Tullibardine en 1394, en Perth, Perthshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 26 de octubre de 1460, en su pueblo, a la edad de 82 años.
